label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question. So the question is asking why some groups support the new law in Virginia regarding the ignition interlock devices. The article says that groups like Mothers Against Drunk Driving and the Washington Regional Alcohol Program support the law. The reason given is that Virginia's alcohol-related road deaths and injuries were still unacceptably high. They also mention that ignition interlock devices reduce repeat offenses. So the support is because they believe this law will help reduce the number of deaths and injuries caused by drunk driving. Now, looking at the options: A) the government can be financed to build roads – The article doesn't mention anything about funding for roads. The cost mentioned is about the installation fee for the device, not government financing for infrastructure. So this is probably not correct. B) some traffic deaths and injuries may be avoided – This aligns with what the supporting groups said. They want to reduce the high number of deaths and injuries. The ignition devices are supposed to prevent drunk driving, which would help avoid those incidents. So this seems correct. C) lower-income drivers will not afford to drink again – The article does mention that lower-income drivers might be affected by the fees, but this is cited as a concern by opponents, not a reason for support. The supporters aren't arguing that drivers can't afford to drink; they’re focused on safety. So this is likely incorrect. D) the court system is forced to work more effectively – The article talks about the court system being burdened with more cases as a concern from the other side. The supporters don’t
